questions.question: The heart is located in which body cavity?
questions.explanation: The heart sits in the mediastinum — the central compartment of the thoracic cavity, between the lungs, posterior to the sternum, and anterior to the vertebral column.

questions.question: The right lung has three lobes while the left lung has two lobes.
questions.explanation: Right lung: 3 lobes (superior, middle, inferior) separated by horizontal and oblique fissures. Left lung: 2 lobes (superior, inferior) separated by the oblique fissure. The left is smaller to accommodate the heart (cardiac notch).

questions.question: The largest organ in the human body by surface area is the ___.
questions.explanation: The skin (integumentary system) covers ~1.5-2.0 m² in adults and weighs ~4kg. It provides protection, thermoregulation, sensation, and vitamin D synthesis. The largest internal organ is the liver.

questions.question: Match each organ to its primary function:

right: Filter blood, produce urine, regulate electrolytes
Filter blood, immune function, old red blood cell recycling

questions.explanation: Kidneys: filtration + homeostasis. Liver: metabolic hub (500+ functions). Pancreas: dual endocrine (hormones) + exocrine (enzymes). Spleen: blood filter + immune reservoir.

questions.question: Arrange the path of blood flow through the heart (starting from systemic return):
questions.explanation: Deoxygenated blood: vena cava → right atrium → right ventricle → pulmonary arteries → lungs. Oxygenated blood: pulmonary veins → left atrium → left ventricle → aorta → body. Two circuits: pulmonary and systemic.

questions.question: Should organ donation be opt-out (presumed consent) rather than opt-in?
questions.scenario: Countries with opt-out systems (Spain, Austria) have 40-50% higher donation rates than opt-in countries (US, UK pre-2020). Thousands die annually waiting for transplants. UK switched to opt-out in 2020.

options.label: Opt-out — saves thousands of lives; people can still opt out if they choose
options.framework: Utilitarian + libertarian paternalism — default saves lives, choice preserved

options.label: Opt-in only — bodily autonomy requires explicit affirmative consent
options.framework: Autonomy — no assumptions about body use

options.label: Mandated choice — everyone must declare a preference (neither default)
options.framework: Active choice — force engagement with the decision

options.label: Opt-out with family veto — presume consent but allow family override
options.framework: Compromise — respect both individual and family

questions.explanation: Evidence strongly favors opt-out: Spain's system achieves the world's highest donation rate. Inertia (not objection) is why most people don't register in opt-in systems. Opt-out respects autonomy (can still refuse) while saving lives through a better default.
